x-enumeration-literals.name: Pledged Collateral Allocation
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Collateral is specifically pledged by the borrower to secure a particular obligation.

Comment:
Example: A borrower pledges €50,000 worth of government bonds to secure a loan.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Floating Collateral Allocation
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Collateral is not tied to a specific obligation but can cover multiple or future obligations.

Comment:
Example: A company provides a pool of inventory as collateral that can secure any short-term credit line drawn.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Margin-Based Collateral Allocation
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Collateral amount is allocated based on required margin or risk coverage levels.

Comment:
Example: In a derivative contract, €10,000 must be allocated as collateral to maintain a 10% margin requirement.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Segmented Collateral Allocation
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Only a portion of an asset or asset pool is allocated to secure an obligation.

Comment:
Example: 60% of a warehouses stock is allocated to secure a loan, leaving 40% free for other purposes.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Priority-Based Collateral Allocation
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Collateral is allocated according to a priority or seniority of obligations.

Comment:
Example: High-priority debts are secured first with pledged assets; remaining assets secure lower-priority obligations.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Time-Limited Collateral Allocation
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Collateral is allocated for a defined period or until a specific condition is met.

Comment:
Example: Securities are allocated to cover a loan only until the loan matures or is repaid.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Cross-Collateral Allocation
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Collateral is used to secure multiple obligations across different accounts or loans.

Comment:
Example: A companys property secures both a term loan and an overdraft facility.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Revolving Collateral Allocation
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Collateral allocation can rotate between obligations as they are drawn and repaid.

Comment:
Example: A bank uses a revolving pool of corporate bonds to back multiple revolving credit facilities.
